tldr: Lob and Sonner are not substitutes — Lob is a direct-mail API (server-side), Sonner is an open-source React toast notification library (client-side UI) — so a 'migration' only makes sense for in-app user feedback, not for any actual mail/messaging workflow.
why_switch: Sonner is free and open source — zero vendor lock-in
Tiny API and great DX for in-app toast notifications
Right tool if the real need is UI feedback, not outbound messaging
Removes a paid vendor from the stack for any UI-only use case
Works natively with React without a backend service
what_to_export: Inventory of places in the app where Lob is currently triggered for user feedback (if any)
Any client-side success/error states tied to Lob calls
Translations / i18n strings used in notifications
List of API integrations that should remain on Lob (mail flows)
export_method: There is no direct data export here because Sonner does not store anything — it is a UI library. Audit your codebase for places where Lob is being used purely to surface in-app messages, and document the copy and triggering events. Consult Sonner's and Lob's official docs for current capabilities.
import_method: Install Sonner in your React app (per its official docs), mount its Toaster component near the app root, and replace the relevant client-side notification calls with Sonner's toast API. Keep Lob in place for any actual outbound mail workflows. Consult Sonner's official docs for current install and usage steps.
effort: 1-3 hours (only if Lob was misused for UI feedback)
gotchas: These are different categories — do not remove Lob if you actually need to send physical mail
Sonner runs in the browser; it cannot deliver email, SMS, or mail
Accessibility: ensure toasts respect screen-reader and reduced-motion settings
Toasts disappear — don't use them for critical confirmations
Keep server-side logging for anything previously tracked through Lob

email.subject: Replacing in-app Lob feedback with Sonner toasts
email.body:
Hi team,

Quick note: where we're currently using Lob purely to drive in-app user feedback, we should switch those spots to Sonner — a free open-source React toast library. Lob stays in place for any real mail/messaging flows.

Scope: audit UI-only call sites, install Sonner, mount the Toaster, and replace those calls with toast.success / toast.error. Estimated 1-3 hours.

Thanks
